description_long: This is a vintage Hitachi television with a boxy, rectangular form and a faux woodgrain finish on the top and sides. The front features a large, curved glass cathode ray tube (CRT) screen set within a thick, dark bezel, and a control panel on the right side with two prominent rotary dials and a speaker grille below. The dials are ribbed for grip and are accompanied by printed labels and a small colored logo, adding to the retro aesthetic. The back of the television is black, protruding outward in a trapezoidal shape to accommodate the CRT's depth, and is likely made of textured plastic with ventilation slots. The overall construction appears robust, with a combination of wood-patterned laminate, metal, and plastic materials. This television would typically be found in older urban homes, garages, or as discarded electronics on sidewalks.
description: A vintage Hitachi CRT television with a woodgrain finish, large curved glass screen, and side control dials. The design is boxy and robust, with a prominent black rear housing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large curved screen, woodgrain bezel, and two rotary dials with a speaker grille on the right.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the woodgrain finish and the protruding black rear housing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the deep, black plastic housing for the CRT and ventilation slots.
description_view_3: Right side view with woodgrain finish and a clear view of the CRT's rear extension.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, dark brown woodgrain bezel with a matte finish and rectangular styling.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of two rotary dials and a button on the front right panel
